mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined z-score of RSAFS and INDPRO shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of housing market stress (HOUST below 1400), a rise in industrial production (INDPRO) fails to translate into broader economic expansion due to supply chain bottlenecks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the mechanism reverses direction during housing booms.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) indicate increased household liquidity buffers that support consumer spending, which positively drives corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no monotonic relationship with RSAFS growth rates, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI exceeds 265 index level, persistent inflation erodes real discount rates and forces aggressive Fed tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses the 265 threshold, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and weakening housing starts (HOUST) signals a misallocation of capital toward services over construction, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-product term of PAYEMS and HOUST shows no incremental predictive power for 5-day returns beyond their individual effects, or in-sample R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeds 3 percent annualized, a subsequent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals peak demand exhaustion, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns when GDPC1 growth decelerates but remains positive, or forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline during identified late-cycle windows.
